mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals immediate labor market deterioration and recession fears, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and rising retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative or flat when both GDPC1 and RSAFS show positive quarter-over-quarter growth, or interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and reduced economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with a hit rate below 45% over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, high borrowing costs compress profit margins and trigger rapid portfolio rebalancing away from equities, causing negative returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of observations where FEDFUNDS surpasses 5.0%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with falling unemployment rates signals a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect improved consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: HOUST increases show no positive correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates fragile supply chains and weak demand, prompting immediate equity market declines within the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances following a >0.5% monthly drop in INDPRO during recovery phases, invalidating the predictive relationship.
